reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Grounded in code: models/ms-marco-reranker/worker.js loads the REAL transformers.js text-classification pipeline (Xenova/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2, WASM, q8) through the shared loadPipeline() and tokenises every (query, passage) pair TOGETHER via text_pair and calls model(enc), reading the RAW single relevance logit off out.logits (num_labels=1) rather than letting the pipeline squash it through a sigmoid — a genuine on-device forward pass, never a canned result. The immutable conformance suite tests this seam: calls-real-inference (source must match pipeline(|createModelLoader|extractor|classifier|reranker — pass) and loads-clean (console-clean, no uncaught errors on load — pass). A genuine CROSS-ENCODER: attention flows across [CLS] query [SEP] passage [SEP], so it judges relevance rather than comparing independent embeddings.
rubric.notes: Retrospective backfill: mechanism verified from source + the existing conformance suite; no fresh latency/output numbers were re-measured in this pass (none fabricated).

rubric.evidence: Expected output = one relevance logit per query–passage pair (out.logits, last dim = num_labels = 1); the page ranks passages by that logit (higher = more relevant) and also shows its sigmoid as a 0–100% bar. A naive lexical-overlap score is computed alongside as the contrast. Conformance io-shape-semantic is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The 'see inside' contrast ranks the same passages by the cross-encoder vs by word-overlap, showing the model reward meaning over shared words.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2), the real pipeline task (text-classification), backend (WASM — no WebGPU required), quantisation (q8), download size (~90 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). Conformance declares-model-id / declares-task / declares-backend / declares-quantisation all pass against these strings.
rubric.notes: At-a-glance is explicit that text-classification is used as a cross-encoder reranker with num_labels=1 — no invented reranker API.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'Xenova/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:90, requiresWebGPU:false}); a valid current on-device cache auto-initialises with no Load click, and only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance shared-auto-init-loader (source createModelLoader) passes; auto-init-on-cached is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 so the ~90 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/running/done state is announced in a role=status aria-live=polite region; failures surface ON THE PAGE as an .err status ('Rerank failed: …'), not only the console; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path. Runs on the WASM path everywhere, so runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds (no GPU gate). Conformance errors-on-the-page + live-status-region pass; honest-progress-retry-offline is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Scored 4 (not 5): progress/retry/offline are honestly wired but were not re-exercised offline in this retrospective backfill.

rubric.evidence: Real keyboard-operable controls: the query <input>, the one-passage-per-line textarea, and the Rerank passages button; results render as a ranked list with score bars. No dead controls.
rubric.notes: Scored 4: controls read as real and keyboard-operable from source, but tap-target sizing has an open automated flag (see accessibility).

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the ranking, the raw logit, the sigmoid bar and the lexical contrast are all derived from the real model logits returned by the worker. Conformance no-fake-output is manual-evidenced.

Every button exposes an accessible name (conformance buttons-have-names pass) and status uses role=status aria-live. The run status is a role=status aria-live line above the results. HOWEVER models.json records an automated pre-scan flag for this demo: desktop 11 + mobile 5 sub-24px target(s) — sub-24px tap targets an agent still has to verify, and support is 'needs-review' on both classes. Honest weakness, not yet cleared.
rubric.notes: Light+dark use the WCAG-AA shared design system. The sub-24px tap-target flag is the reason this is 4/minor, not 5.

rubric.evidence: The immutable suite carries responsive-desktop and responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) assertions and the shared design system collapses panels to one column on narrow viewports. BUT support.desktop and support.mobile are both 'needs-review' (not yet confirmed 'ok' by an agent matrix pass) and the automated scan flagged desktop 11 + mobile 5 sub-24px target(s).
rubric.notes: Scored 4: parity is plausibly fine but UNVERIFIED in this backfill; parity-both-classes remains manual-evidenced/awaiting an agent verdict.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated Web Worker; the page renders only bounded reductions (a per-pair logit ranking and score bars over a handful of passages) — no main-thread inference and no unbounded per-token loop. Conformance inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced; inference-off-main-thread (source) passes.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — the model forward pass never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work to web worker; break up long tasks so the UI stays responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive (INP): move heavy work off the main thread and avoid long blocking tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — every inference call runs in a dedicated module Web Worker (worker.js) via loadPipeline(); the page only posts messages and renders results, so tokenisation + the model forward pass never block the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread pipeline; conformance inference-off-main-thread (source new Worker|worker.js) pass + inp-stays-low

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long synchronous loops and DOM updates to keep the main thread free
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break heavy synchronous processing / DOM updates so the browser can handle input and repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the heavy work (model forward pass) is already off-thread in the worker; the on-page reductions (similarity matrix / projection / score rendering) are bounded over a small number of items, not unbounded per-token loops on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: bounded on-main-thread rendering; worker owns the model pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout flexible grid no horizontal overflow intrinsic sizing for a control panel and results
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the shared design system (public/styles.css) lays the panels out with flexible grids that collapse to one column on narrow viewports; results scroll inside their own overflow-x container rather than pushing the page wide.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are in the immutable suite

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status announce loading ready error with role=status aria-live; every control has an accessible name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; use semantic HTML.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the run status uses role=status aria-live=polite, the shared model-loader announces checking/initialising/ready/error, and every button carries a text label.

openQuestions: support.desktop/mobile are 'needs-review' — confirm tap-target sizing and flip support to ok.
Should the raw logit vs sigmoid distinction be surfaced more prominently for non-expert users?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass to verify tap targets and clear the sub-24px flag on both classes.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded critique for ms-marco-reranker (Xenova/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2, text-classification used as a num_labels=1 cross-encoder, WASM q8, ~90 MB, Apache-2.0). Real off-thread inference reads the RAW relevance logit off each (query,passage) pair (not a canned score), ranks by it, and contrasts with naive word-overlap — verified from source + the immutable conformance suite. Open weakness: support 'needs-review' + sub-24px tap-target flag (accessibility/responsive 4/minor). No fabricated numbers.
